newsSince: No new fundamental company-specific news since June 9. The two binary events remain on the calendar: the June 26 redomicile/reincorporation shareholder vote (removes PFIC overhang, key catalyst) and the June 29 securities class-action lead-plaintiff deadline (overhang/risk). Q1 revenue was $0.50M with a $12.3M net loss; the ~$400M AI data-center production expansion plan is ongoing.

sources.label: POET June crucible: redomicile vote and class action

verdicts.rankRationale: On the CPO asymmetry lens, no new fundamental damage since June 9; the optionality thesis is intact into the June 26 redomicile vote (removes PFIC overhang, could unlock US institutional demand). Slight positive drift for catalyst proximity, but June 29 class-action deadline caps conviction. Hold at 11.
verdicts.bull: June 26 redomicile vote strips PFIC overhang; pure-play optical interposer/CPO optionality with very high upside if 800G engines scale.
verdicts.neutral: Pre-scale revenue (~$0.5M Q1); binary event-driven name where the vote and litigation define near-term path.
verdicts.bear: June 29 class-action lead-plaintiff deadline, customer/order uncertainty (Celestial/Marvell fallout) and disclosure risk; deep losses.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: June 26 redomicile vote and ~$400M AI data-center capacity expansion.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Securities class action and pre-scale execution/order uncertainty.
